context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct WooCommerce Subscriptions filter for proration, implements proper daily-rate proration calculation, enforces a non-negative floor, and uses the correct programmatic plan-switch sequence with calculate_totals and save.

checklist.name: Correct proration filter
checklist.description: Uses the woocommerce_subscriptions_switch_proration filter (not a custom hook or direct function override)

checklist.name: Filter parameter count
checklist.description: The proration filter callback accepts all 5 parameters: $proration_amount, $subscription, $new_order, $product, $switch_cart_item with priority 10

checklist.name: Next payment date used
checklist.description: Uses $subscription->get_next_payment_date() to determine the end of the billing period

checklist.name: Last payment date used
checklist.description: Uses $subscription->get_date('last_payment') to determine the start of the billing period

checklist.name: Daily rate calculation
checklist.description: Calculates both old and new daily rates by dividing plan price by the number of days in the billing period

checklist.name: Days remaining used
checklist.description: Multiplies the rate difference by remaining days (not total days) to produce the proration amount

checklist.name: Non-negative floor
checklist.description: Ensures the returned proration amount is never less than 0.00 (uses max(), a conditional, or similar guard)

checklist.name: Rounded result
checklist.description: Returns the proration amount rounded to 2 decimal places using round()

checklist.name: wcs_get_subscription null-check
checklist.description: The plan-switch function calls wcs_get_subscription() and returns early (false/null) if the result is falsy

checklist.name: Remove existing items
checklist.description: Iterates over subscription items and calls remove_item() on each before adding the new product

checklist.name: WC_Order_Item_Product used
checklist.description: Creates a WC_Order_Item_Product instance and uses set_product() and set_quantity() to configure it

checklist.name: calculate_totals and save
checklist.description: Calls $subscription->calculate_totals() followed by $subscription->save() after adding the new item
